title: Minimum Viable Contract
description: Machine-readable schema for the minimum viable capability contract described in the toolkit spec.

id.description: Semantic capability identifier in lowercase slash-delimited form such as payment/retry or auth/session/login.

version.description: Semantic version number for the contract using MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH.

owner.description: The team or individual accountable for maintaining this contract.

description.description: Business intent for the capability: what it does and why it exists.

inputs.description: Declared inputs that the capability accepts, along with their business constraints.

outputs.description: Declared outputs that the capability returns, along with the guarantees each output provides.

non_goals.description: Explicit statements of what the capability does not do.

use_cases.description: Business intents or practitioner scenarios that this capability satisfies.

open_questions.description: Unresolved decisions that must be closed before the contract can be treated as authoritative.

name.description: Input field name used by the capability.

type.description: Declared JSON-compatible type for the input.

required.description: Whether this input must be provided by callers.

constraints.description: Business constraint on this input and why that constraint exists.

name.description: Output field name produced by the capability.

type.description: Declared JSON-compatible type for the output.

guarantees.description: Guarantee this capability makes about the output value.

nonGoal.description: Single-sentence statement of what the capability explicitly does not do.

useCase.description: A practitioner-facing scenario that this capability serves.

openQuestion.description: A question that still needs resolution before the contract can be authoritative.
